spec_match.rationale: Demo touches the feature at a conceptual level but does not exercise the full spec surface (edge cases, modes, or all properties the spec defines).

interactive.rationale: No interactive controls of substance — the page reads as a static code card, which CLAUDE.md rule 7 explicitly forbids.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates and delivers on the framing used in the feature index card.

edge_cases.rationale: Some unsupported / error handling is wired up, but several spec edge cases (boundary inputs, alternate modes, error states) are not surfaced.

references.rationale: Page cites the required chromestatus.com/feature/6436406437871616 self-heal link (CLAUDE.md rule 3).

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex of note, and button overrides keep background + color paired (CLAUDE.md rules 5 and 6).

openQuestions.title: Add a real interactive control surface
openQuestions.detail: The concept page currently reads more like a write-up than a hands-on demo. Add a control that drives the underlying API (or a faithful simulation) and surfaces the live result on the page — buttons, sliders, selects, or a try-it form. CLAUDE.md rule 7 explicitly bans static code cards as concept pages.

openQuestions.title: Exercise more of the WebGPU Compatibility mode spec surface
openQuestions.detail: The spec (https://github.com/gpuweb/gpuweb/blob/main/proposals/compatibility-mode.md) covers more than the page demonstrates. Walk back through the spec's exposed methods, properties, or modes and add controls for the ones that materially change behaviour. Today the page only hints at a subset.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate failure and edge-case branches
openQuestions.detail: Add visible branches for the unsupported / unavailable / error / boundary inputs paths. For built-in AI APIs, that means 'downloadable', 'downloading', 'unavailable' availability states; for HTTP features, error responses and CSP blocks; for CSS features, zero / extreme / inherited values. Today only the happy path is reachable from the UI.

openQuestions.title: Mirror the spec's stated motivation in the demo
openQuestions.detail: ChromeStatus motivation reads: 'WebGPU is a good match for modern graphics APIs such as Vulkan, Metal and Direct3D 12. However, there are a large number of devices which do not yet support those APIs. In particular, on Chrome on Windows, 31% of Chrome users do not have Di...'. The demo should make that motivation visible — show the problem the spec is solving (before/after, with-without, attacker view) so a reader leaves understanding why the feature ships, not just what it does.

summary: 'Compatibility Limits' misses a non-trivial part of the rubric (1 fail / 2 partial). The open questions list captures the highest-leverage fixes — usually adding a real interactive control or wiring the chromestatus reference link.
